title-group.article-title: Moments of derivatives of quadratic Dirichlet L-functions with prime conductor

contrib-group.aff: https://ror.org/03yghzc09grid.8391.30000 0004 1936 8024Department of Mathematics, University of Exeter, Exeter, EX4 4QF United Kingdom

license.license-p: Open Access This article is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License, which permits use, sharing, adaptation, distribution and reproduction in any medium or format, as long as you give appropriate credit to the original author(s) and the source, provide a link to the Creative Commons licence, and indicate if changes were made. institution-wrap.institution: Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council

sec.title: Introduction
sec.p: Our main result extends the study of these moments of Dirichlet L-functions over prime polynomials and provides an asymptotic formula for the mixed second moment of their derivatives.

sec.title: Theorem 1.1
Theorem 1.2
Background on L-functions over function fields
Preliminary tools
Lemma 2.1
Proof
Lemma 2.2
Proof
sec.p: Lastly, we have the Weil bound for character sums over monic, irreducible polynomials.

sec.title: Lemma 2.3
Proof
The twisted first moment of the k-th derivative
Lemma 3.1
Proof
Proof of Theorem 1.2
Lemma 4.1
Proof
sec.p: We are now ready to prove Theorem 1.1.

sec.title: Proof of Theorem 1.1

fn.p: Springer Nature remains neutral with regard to jurisdictional claims in published maps and institutional affiliations.

ack.title: Acknowledgements
ack.p: I would like to thank my supervisor Julio Andrade for suggesting this problem to me and for his guidance throughout. I would also like to thank the anonymous referees for their helpful comments and suggestions that led to the improvement of the original manuscript. The research of the author was supported by an Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC) studentship [EP/V520317/1] at the University of Exeter. The author has no competing interests to declare that are relevant to the content of this article.

notes.title: Data Availability
notes.p: Data sharing is not applicable to this article as no datasets were generated or analysed for this work.
